pattern: Count the decimal digits of each number and tally those with an even digit count; within the given constraint range only 2-digit and 4-digit numbers qualify.
hints: The number of digits in a positive integer is floor(log10(n)) + 1, or equivalently the length of its string representation.
Even digit counts are 2, 4, 6, … — within the constraint 1 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 10^5 only 2-digit (10-99) and 4-digit (1000-9999) numbers qualify.
Iterate over the array; for each element compute its digit count and increment a counter whenever that count is even.

edge_inputs.description: all single-digit values, answer is zero
maximum value 100000 has 6 digits, which is even

stress.description: random values in the full constraint range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    return [[rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(size)]]
